name: Almond Blondie Triangles
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces white chocolate (chopped into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line an 8-inch square pan with foil, allowing ends to extend above the pan on two sides.
2. Grease the foil in the pan.
3. For the crust: Pulse toasted almonds until finely chopped. Add sugar and butter; pulse until well mixed. Press mixture evenly over the bottom of the lined pan.
4. For Blondie:
a. Melt butter in microwave or saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat, add white chocolate, and let stand 2 minutes. Whisk until smooth.
b. Beat eggs, both sugars, and baking powder in a medium bowl with an electric mixer on high speed until pale and fluffy (about 2 minutes).
c. Add cooled white chocolate, lemon peel, and extracts. Stir in flour until just blended.
5. Spread batter evenly over the crust. Sprinkle with slivered almonds, patting them slightly into the batter.
6. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a pick inserted in the center comes out with moist crumb. Cool on a wire rack.
7. Lift foil by ends onto a cutting board. Cut brownies into 9 squares. Cut each square diagonally.
